summary: Game-cheat package with stealth persistence, hidden supervisor, eval'd obfuscated payload, and HWID exfiltration
details: On require()/CLI start, launcher.js writes the bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs into HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run under the name 'OneDriveUpdate', deliberately impersonating the signed Microsoft OneDrive updater so the package auto-launches at every Windows logon (launcher.js:88, via execSync of a PowerShell Set-ItemProperty call). It then detaches a hidden supervisor process (SENSIVITY_SUPERVISOR=1, windowsHide:true, stdio:'ignore') that respawns a worker whenever it exits and only stops if a.sensivity-stop sentinel is present; both supervisor and worker set process.title = 'Runtime Broker' to impersonate the legitimate Windows component (launcher.js:108, launcher.js:127). The launcher then eval()s the bundled, heavily obfuscated server.obf.js (obfuscator.io transform, 397-entry RC4-decoded string array, control-flow flattening, ~1302 transforms) instead of require()ing it (launcher.js:339). The eval'd code reads the Windows MachineGuid from HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography via PowerShell, combines it with os.hostname() and the C: volume serial to build a stable SHA256 HWID, and POSTs {key, hwid, nonce, app, version} to a hardcoded LICENSE_URL. The shipped 6.8MB native sens.node module and public/index.html expose a game-cheat UI (Aimbot, Triggerbot, ESP, Crosshair, Vehicle Spawn, Teleport), and a YouTube-detection routine gates the cheat panel to evade streamer detection. The combination of Microsoft-impersonating autorun persistence, hidden self-respawning daemon with spoofed process title, eval-of-obfuscated-blob loader, and host-identifier exfiltration is unambiguous malware behavior against any installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
